name: pester-testing
description: Write, review, and run Pester 5 tests in PowerShell with LLM-optimized output.

hooks.command: if(-not(Get-Module Pester -ListAvailable|Where-Object{$_.Version -ge [version]'5.0'})){Write-Warning 'Pester 5 not installed, skipping pre-commit check.';exit 0};$cfg=New-PesterConfiguration;$cfg.Output.Verbosity.Value='Normal';$cfg.Run.PassThru.Value=$true;$r=Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg;if($r.FailedCount -gt 0){Write-Host("Commit blocked: $($r.FailedCount) Pester test(s) failed. Fix before committing.") -ForegroundColor Red;exit 1}

hooks.prompt: The Pester test file $ARGUMENTS was just modified. Run the tests in that specific file using the pester-run skill and report results concisely.
hooks.statusMessage: Running Pester for modified test file...

pester.description: Run Pester 5 tests with agent-optimized output (failures and summary only). Accepts an optional path and tag filter arguments.
pester.content:
Run Pester 5 tests using the pester-run workflow.

## Parsing Arguments

Parse `$ARGUMENTS` if provided:
- A `.Tests.ps1` path → set `$cfg.Run.Path.Value`
- `-Tag <name>` → set `$cfg.Filter.Tag.Value = @('<name>')`
- `-ExcludeTag <name>` → set `$cfg.Filter.ExcludeTag.Value = @('<name>')`
- No arguments → run all `*.Tests.ps1` files discovered from the current directory

## Execution

Build a `New-PesterConfiguration` with `Output.Verbosity.Value = 'Normal'` and `Run.PassThru.Value = $true`. Apply any filters. Run with `Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg`.

## Reporting

- All pass: `All N tests passed (X.Xs)`
- Failures: one line per failure showing the full test path and assertion error, then the summary line (`Passed: N | Failed: N | Skipped: N`)

monitors.name: pester-file-watcher
monitors.command: mkdir -p "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}" && stamp="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/watch-stamp"; touch "$stamp"; while true; do sleep 2; find . -name '*.Tests.ps1' -newer "$stamp" 2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r f; do echo "Test file changed: $f"; done; touch "$stamp"; done
monitors.description: Watch for changes to Pester test files (*.Tests.ps1) and deliver a notification on each change.

coverageThreshold.title: Code Coverage Threshold (%)
coverageThreshold.description: Minimum acceptable code coverage percentage. When greater than 0, Pester enables coverage tracking and warns if results fall below this value. Set to 0 to disable.

psesPath.title: PowerShell Editor Services Bundle Path
psesPath.description: Path to the directory containing the PowerShellEditorServices folder. Found at ~/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode.powershell-<ver>/modules, or extract from https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShellEditorServices/releases. Leave blank to disable LSP.
